steps.title: Identify the observable quality contrast
steps.content: The passage opens by noting handedness is “easy to observe in humans,” then states the trait is present but “less ______” in other animals, prompting researchers to use special tasks. The blank must name the reduced version of that same observable quality.

steps.title: Select the word that echoes observability
steps.content:
“Recognizable” directly mirrors the “easy to observe” quality from the opening—handedness is recognizable in humans but less recognizable in animals, explaining why special detection tasks are necessary.

The other choices address unrelated properties: interest level, importance, and functionality.

That matches Choice A — recognizable.
